asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover, wide landscape hero and 16:9 article-card crop
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover, wide landscape 3:2
Primary request: Create a clean editorial smart-home photograph about the homeowner decision between fresh-air ventilation for elevated carbon dioxide and room filtration for fine particles.
Scene/backdrop: A calm, warm modern living room with natural oak, cream linen, a wood-framed window, and soft residential detail. The window is partly open to clean daylight. A physically plausible, simple unbranded portable HEPA-style air purifier sits naturally on the floor elsewhere in the same room; it must look like an ordinary real appliance category, not a fictional branded product or futuristic gadget.
Subject: The room itself is the subject. A homeowner is present only as a natural partial figure or hand near the window latch, deciding what action to take. No screen or monitor is the focal point.
Style/medium: Photorealistic editorial interior photography with believable materials and imperfect everyday texture, not a render or diagram.
Composition/framing: Wide horizontal frame suitable for a blog hero and a shorter article-card crop; both the open window and purifier remain recognizable near the central safe crop. No headline space is required. No split screen.
Lighting/mood: Soft late-morning natural light, calm, practical, reassuring, realistic.
Color palette: Warm off-white, oak, linen, with restrained dark-green accents in one cushion or small household detail.
Constraints: No text, labels, logos, brand marks, watermark, visible fake data, phone UI, computer UI, smart-home dashboard, chart, graph, arrows, lines, routes, network motifs, translucent panels, inset frames, floating cards, progress bars, or decorative overlays. No smoke haze. Do not imply the purifier removes carbon dioxide. Do not imitate a specific commercial model. Keep all architecture and appliance geometry physically plausible.
Avoid: generic AI diagram aesthetics, glossy futuristic styling, staged showroom perfection, invented branded hardware, floating objects, duplicated objects, warped hands, excessive plants.
